meta.description: Companion to agent_initiated_file_read_provenance: scripts the NON-compliant call shape the 'Agent-initiated file reads' clause forbids — mcp_neotoma_store called with a bare source_file citation and no file_path/asset_entity_id, no EMBEDS relationship ever created for the read file. This is the failure mode issue #1933 reports (dangling source_file citation, file_urls resolves to []). NOTE (per PR #1934 qa-lens review, Phoenicurus): as of this fixture, packages/cursor-hooks/hooks/_common.ts has no detection logic for this case — missed_steps/turn_diagnosis are populated by the hook's own diagnosis code, which does not yet classify a dangling source_file citation. This fixture intentionally does NOT assert a turn_compliance/turn_diagnosis flag for that reason; it pins only the harness-plumbing behavior that is true today — the non-compliant mcp_neotoma_store call (bare source_file, no file_path, no asset_entity_id) is captured verbatim in the tool_invocation's input_summary/output. It deliberately does not assert an exact /create_relationship request count, since any such count would reflect incidental hook bookkeeping (REFERS_TO/PART_OF for message linking), not a semantic check for the missing EMBEDS link. Asserting behavioral detection of the missing EMBEDS link is deferred to the schema_registry.ts soft guard tracked in the eval-coverage fast-follow issue linked from PR #1934.

payload.prompt: Read /Users/agent/workspace/lab_results.pdf and store the lab readings you find in it.

payload.text: Stored 2 lab readings from lab_results.pdf (Glucose 95 mg/dL, Cholesterol 180 mg/dL).
